Dalgo uses Vaultwarden as the central, secure place to store and share sensitive credentials across teams and client projects. This includes items such as data warehouse/database credentials, SSH keys, API keys, and other access details required for implementation, maintenance, and support.

In some cases, you may need direct access to your data warehouse from your own machine. This is useful for inspecting tables directly, checking whether data is ready for charting, validating transformed outputs, and reviewing dbt model logic to confirm that the data pipeline is behaving as expected.

Dalgo supports visualisation through native dashboards and, optionally, a hosted Superset instance for more advanced dashboarding.

Within your Dalgo subscription, you can use: Dalgo Native Dashboards — build charts and dashboards directly inside Dalgo, with no external tool required. Superset — a hosted Superset instance for customisable, role-based visualisations. Available to organisations on the Dalgo + Superset plan.

Dalgo also works with any external BI or dashboarding tool of your choice. Connect your data warehouse to your preferred tool — such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Metabase — and start building. Note: Dalgo does not manage or host external BI tools. You bring your own licence or account and connect it to the warehouse that Dalgo pipelines into.

Dalgo's built-in dashboards let you build charts from your data and arrange them into shareable views — no separate BI tool required. You can create multiple dashboards for different audiences, such as a field team view and a donor report.
Select Dashboards on the left menu panel. You will see all dashboards in your organisation. Use the search bar to find a dashboard by name.

Select + Create Dashboard. Dalgo immediately creates a new empty dashboard and opens the dashboard builder. In the builder, select + Add Chart to browse and add existing charts to the canvas. Drag charts to reposition them. Drag the edges or corners to resize. Select Save when you are done. info Dalgo creates a draft dashboard the moment you select + Create Dashboard. If you leave without saving, the draft is kept — you can continue editing it from the dashboard list.

Any dashboard can be set as the first screen you see when you log in. Open the dashboard you want to use. Select Set as my landing page from the dashboard menu. The dashboard will now appear on your Impact at a Glance home screen every time you log in.
documents.s: Setting a dashboard as your home page

documents.t: Charts are created separately and then added to dashboards.

Select Charts on the left menu panel. Select + New Chart. Choose a chart type: Bar Chart — compare values across categories Pie Chart — show proportions of a whole Line Chart — display trends over time Number — show a single metric value Map — visualise data by geography Table — display raw data in rows and columns Select the dataset (database table) your chart will draw from, then select Configure. In the configuration screen, choose which columns to use for axes, values, filters, and labels. Give your chart a name and select Save. The chart is now available to add to any dashboard.
Select Charts on the left menu panel. Find the chart you want to edit and select the ⋯ menu. Select Edit. Make your changes and select Save.

Superset is available as an optional hosted dashboarding environment within Dalgo for organisations that need more flexible and role-based visualisations. note Superset is only available if your organisation has subscribed to the Dalgo + Superset plan. Contact support@dalgo.org if you want to add it to your subscription.

Select Dashboards on the left menu panel. If Superset is enabled for your organisation, select the Open Superset option. A sign-in prompt will appear. Select Sign in with Google. If your email has been granted access, you will be logged in and Superset will open. Once logged in, your warehouse data is already connected. You can immediately start building charts, dashboards, and applying role-based access controls inside Superset. note If your email has not been granted access to Superset, contact your organisation's Superset administrator or reach out to support@dalgo.org.

Ingest is where you connect your data sources and warehouse so Dalgo can automatically pull in your data. The Ingest page has three tabs: Connections — link a source to your warehouse to start syncing data (shown by default) Sources — add the external systems your data comes from (e.g. Kobo Toolbox, Google Sheets) Your Warehouse — connect the database where your data will be stored (e.g. BigQuery or Postgres) If you are setting up for the first time, work through them in this order: Your Warehouse → Sources → Connections. note You may need to whitelist these IP addresses in your organisation's firewall before Dalgo can connect to your data sources: 13.202.128.47 65.2.173.97

A connection tells Dalgo what data to sync from a saved source into your warehouse. If a source answers "where does the data live?", a connection answers "which data should be copied into the warehouse, and how should it sync?".
Select Ingest on the left menu panel. The Connections tab is shown by default. You will see a list of all connections in your organisation.

Select + Add Connection. Give your connection a name and select the source you want to sync. The available tables or data streams from that source will appear. Select which streams (tables) you want to sync. Choose the sync mode for each stream. Select Connect to save. The first sync will start automatically. To test your connection, select the Sync button on the connection row. Sync logs will appear below.

From each connection row you can: Sync now — trigger an immediate sync using the sync icon View history — see past sync runs and their status using the history icon Edit — change stream selection or settings using the ⋯ menu Delete — remove the connection using the ⋯ menu warning Deleting a connection is permanent and removes all its sync history. This cannot be undone.

If columns or fields change in your source data, Dalgo detects those changes automatically. When a change is detected, a Pending Changes section appears in the Connections tab listing the affected connections. Select View next to a connection to review what changed. You can approve or ignore the changes. Approving updates the connection so future syncs use the new schema. note If a critical field (such as a cursor field) is removed, Dalgo treats it as a breaking change that cannot be approved in Dalgo. The issue must be fixed at the source first. If you are unsure how schema changes affect your downstream models or dashboards, contact support@dalgo.org.

A source is any system where your data already lives. Common examples include KoboToolbox, Google Sheets, CommCare, Avni, Zoho, and many others. When you add a source in Dalgo, you are saving the access credentials for that system. After that, you create a connection to choose which data syncs into your warehouse.
Select Ingest on the left menu panel. Select the Sources tab. You will see a list of all sources added to your organisation. Use the search bar to find a source by name or type.

Select + Add Source. A dialog will appear. Search for or select the Source Type (e.g. KoboToolbox, Google Sheets). Enter a Source Name. Fill in the required credentials or connection details. The fields shown depend on the source type. If you are unsure what credentials are needed, check the provider's documentation or contact the person who manages that system. Select Save Changes and Test. Dalgo will confirm it can reach the source. Select Save once the test succeeds.

On the Sources tab, select the ⋯ menu on the source row. Select Edit to update credentials and test again, or Delete to remove the source. note You cannot change the source type of an existing source. If you need a different source type, create a new source instead. warning Deleting a source also removes any connections that depend on it. This cannot be undone.

Your warehouse is the single location where data from all your sources is stored. Dalgo supports BigQuery and PostgreSQL as warehouse destinations. You need to set up your warehouse before adding sources or connections.

Select Ingest on the left menu panel. Select the Your Warehouse tab. Select the type of warehouse from the Destination type dropdown — either BigQuery or Postgres. Enter a name for your warehouse in the Name field. Fill in the connection details. The fields depend on the destination type: BigQuery — requires a project ID and service account JSON credentials Postgres — requires a host, port, database name, username, and password Select Test Connection. Dalgo will verify it can reach your warehouse. Select Save once the test succeeds.

To update your warehouse credentials or switch to a different warehouse: Select the Your Warehouse tab. Select the Edit button. Make your changes, test the connection, and save. note If you need help obtaining warehouse credentials, contact your internal tech team, your tech partner, or the Dalgo team at support@dalgo.org.

Our open-source data platform enables NGOs to automate data consolidation, transformation, storage and visualisation through a unified interface. ⏰ Spend no time on repetitive manual data crunching 📊 Direct your efforts towards the use of data to monitor and evaluate your impact 📣 Learn, iterate and communicate your impact internally and externally Visit dalgo.org to learn more about the product and pricing, or contact us at support@dalgo.org. Our team is always available on Discord. Join our server and chat with us on Dalgo Support.

insights.dalgo.org is the interface for your M&E team, data analysts, or IT team. It enables you to set up and monitor automated data pipelines through the following sections: Ingest — set up your data warehouse, connect to your data sources, and link those sources to your warehouse. Transform — connect to your dbt repository, which contains the SQL code for cleaning, merging, and computing your data. Orchestrate — schedule your data ingestion and transformation to run automatically. Dashboards — explore your data through Dalgo Native Dashboards or Superset. Reports — capture and share snapshots of your dashboards for routine monitoring and donor updates. Pipeline Overview — monitor the health of your pipelines and view past run history. User Management — add team members and assign roles to control their access. Note: Superset is only available if you have subscribed to the Dalgo + Superset plan.

When you log in, you land on the Impact at a Glance home screen. If a dashboard has been set as your landing page, it appears here automatically. If no landing page is configured yet, you will see two options: Choose a dashboard — select an existing dashboard to set as your home screen Create new dashboard — build a new dashboard from scratch To set a landing page, open any dashboard and select Set as my landing page from the dashboard menu.

Your data pipelines will typically already be set up for you by the Dalgo team or one of our partners. The steps below help you log in and start using the platform. You will receive an invitation email from notifications@dalgo.org. Select the link in the email to open the Dalgo platform. Accept the invitation and set up your password. You are now logged in. If the Pipeline Overview says "No pipelines available", reach out to support@dalgo.org or the partner helping with your setup.
documents.s: Getting started as a first-time user

documents.t: Dalgo's data quality feature uses Elementary to monitor your dbt models and surface data issues automatically. Elementary is a dbt observability package that runs alongside your transformations. Once set up, it shows you test results, anomalies, and model run history across all your data models.

Select Data Quality on the left menu panel. What you see depends on your setup: If dbt is not configured — you will see a message asking you to set up your Transform workspace first. Go to Transform to get started. If Elementary is not yet set up — you will see a one-time setup prompt. Select Set up Elementary to add it to your dbt project. If Elementary is set up — the full report loads automatically.
When you first enable Elementary, Dalgo installs and configures the package in your dbt project.

Once Elementary is running, the Data Quality page shows: Test results — pass/fail status for each dbt test across your models Model runs — when each model last ran and whether it succeeded Anomalies — automatically detected irregularities in row counts, freshness, or column values note Results update each time your pipeline runs transformation tasks. If you have not run a pipeline recently, the report may not show your latest data. The exact checks available in your Dalgo environment may vary by organisation and pipeline. If you need help interpreting a failing check, contact your data team or support@dalgo.org.

The Pipeline Overview shows a visual history of your data pipeline runs so you can quickly see what ran, when, and whether it succeeded. This is a read-only status view. To manage or schedule pipelines, go to Orchestrate.

Select Pipeline Overview under Managing Data on the left menu panel. You will see a card for each pipeline. Each card shows: The pipeline name A bar chart of recent runs — green bars are successful, red bars are failed, yellow bars indicate the pipeline ran but a dbt test failed The last run time and who triggered it
Select any bar in the chart to expand the run's logs directly below the pipeline card. You will see each task that ran, its duration, and whether it succeeded or failed.

Each pipeline card has a Scale to runtime toggle. When turned on, bar heights are proportional to how long each run took — useful for spotting runs that were unusually slow. When turned off, all bars are the same height and only colour indicates success or failure.

The Usage Dashboard shows how your organisation is using the Dalgo platform — including which dashboards are being viewed, by whom, and how often. note The Usage Dashboard requires a Superset subscription. If you see an error message when opening this page, your organisation may not have Superset enabled. Contact support@dalgo.org for more information.
Select Usage Dashboard under Managing Data on the left menu panel. The dashboard loads automatically as an embedded Superset view.

The Metrics tab shows: Active Users — a bar graph showing the total number of active and inactive users. A user is counted as active if they have visited at least one dashboard. List of users — a table of all users in your organisation with their total dashboard visits. List of dashboards — a table of all dashboards in your organisation with their total visit count. Filters at the top let you view metrics by month, role, and dashboard.

The Trends tab shows: Number of users accessing dashboards — a monthly trendline of how many users are viewing dashboards, with the change compared to the previous month. Visits per user — a monthly trendline of the average number of visits per user, with the change compared to the previous month. Top-level filters allow you to slice trends by role and dashboard.
User Management lets you add team members to your Dalgo organisation and control what each person can see and do.

Select User Management on the left menu panel (under Settings). You will see two tabs: Users — all current members of your organisation and their roles Pending Invitations — invitations that have been sent but not yet accepted

Select Invite User in the top-right corner. Enter the user's Email address. Select a Role from the dropdown. Select Send Invitation. The user will receive an invitation email. They will appear under Pending Invitations until they accept and set up their account.
On the Pending Invitations tab, select the ⋯ menu on an invitation row to: Resend the invitation Delete the invitation

On the Users tab, select the ⋯ menu on the user's row. Select Edit Role to assign a different role, or Remove to revoke their access. warning Removing a user immediately revokes their access to Dalgo. They will not be able to log in until re-invited.
